sentences.base_language: One morning, Khalid woke up with a headache and a stomachache, and felt very tired.
sentences.target_language: في صباحٍ من الأصباح، استيقظ خالد وهو يشعر بصُداعٍ وأَلَمٍ في بطنه، وكان يشعر بتعبٍ شديد.
sentences.romanized: Fī ṣabāḥin min al-aṣbāḥ, istayqaẓa Khālid wa-huwa yashʿuru bi-ṣudāʿin wa-alamin fī baṭnih, wa-kāna yashʿuru bi-taʿabin shadīd.
sentences.grammarNote:
**صُداع** (ṣudāʿ) = headache: note the vowel pattern — the فُعَال (fuʿāl) pattern often signals illness or ailment nouns: **صُداع** (headache), **سُعال** (cough), **زُكام** (cold). The bāʾ of accompaniment (**بِـ**) = with/in: يشعر **بـ**صُداع (feels a headache — lit. feels with a headache). This **بـ** is very common after يشعر and أحسّ.
**وهو يشعر** — circumstantial clause (ḥāl): **وهو** (while he) + present tense creates a ḥāl clause describing the state at the moment of the main verb: استيقظ **وهو يشعر** (woke up while feeling). The ḥāl clause always matches the subject of the main verb and describes its state simultaneously — a key Arabic syntactic feature.

sentences.base_language: Lately he had been working a lot and wasn't sleeping well.
sentences.target_language: كان يعمل كثيرًا في الآونة الأخيرة، ولم يكن ينام بشكلٍ جيد.
sentences.romanized: Kāna yaʿmalu kathīran fī al-āwuna al-akhīra, wa-lam yakun yanāmu bi-shaklin jayyid.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كان + مضارع** = past progressive: **كان يعمل** (was working — lit. was he-works). This is the standard Arabic past progressive, formed by **كان** (past of كون) + present tense verb. The present verb agrees with the subject in gender/number: كانت **تعمل** (she was working), كانوا **يعملون** (they were working).
**لم يكن يَنام** = was not sleeping: **لم** (negative past particle) + **يكن** (jussive of كان) + present tense verb. This stacks negation onto the past progressive: لم **يكن يعمل** (was not working), لم **تكن تنام** (she was not sleeping). **بشكلٍ جيد** = in a good manner, well — an alternative to the adverb **جيدًا**. **في الآونة الأخيرة** = recently/lately — a fixed expression: الآونة (period/time) + الأخيرة (recent).

sentences.base_language: He decided to go to the hospital near his home.
sentences.target_language: قرَّر الذهاب إلى المستشفى القريب من منزله.
sentences.romanized: Qarrara al-dhahāba ilā al-mustashfā al-qarīb min manzilih.
sentences.grammarNote:
**قرَّر + المصدر** = decided to do: قرَّر **الذهاب** (decided to go — decided the-going). After قرَّر (to decide), Arabic uses the **مصدر** (verbal noun) with the definite article: قرَّر **العمل** (decided to work), قرَّر **السفر** (decided to travel). The definite article on the masdar makes it specific — 'the act of going.' Compare: **أن + مضارع** (that he goes): قرَّر **أن يذهب** — both are correct; the masdar is more concise.
**المستشفى** = hospital — note the pattern المُستَفعَل (mustafʿal) — a Form X noun of place: المستشفى (the hospital, a place of seeking healing — from شفى, to heal). Form X (**استفعل**) often carries the meaning 'to seek/request': **استشفى** (sought a cure), **استغفر** (sought forgiveness). **القريب من** = near (adj.): **قريب** (near) takes the preposition **من** (from): قريب **من** المنزل (near the house).

sentences.base_language: He washed his face and got dressed, but felt too weak to eat breakfast.
sentences.target_language: غسَل وجهه وارتدى ملابسه، لكنه شعر بضعفٍ شديد يمنعه من تناول الإفطار.
sentences.romanized: Ghasala wajhahu wa-irtadā malābisah, lākinnahū shaʿara bi-ḍaʿfin shadīd yamnaʿuhū min tanāwul al-ifṭār.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ارتدى** (Form VIII: افتعل) = to put on, to wear: from **ردي** (root ر-د-و). Form VIII verbs (افتعل) often carry a reflexive nuance: **ارتدى** (put on oneself), **اكتسب** (acquired for oneself), **اعتمد** (relied on). Compare: **لبس** (to wear/put on — Form I, simpler): لبس **قميصاً** (wore a shirt) — both are acceptable; **ارتدى** is slightly more formal.
**يمنعه من + مصدر** = prevents him from doing: **يمنع** (present tense, describing an ongoing state) + **ه** (him, suffix) + **من** (from) + **تناول** (masdar of تناول, to take/eat). This relative clause with a present verb is a ḥāl (circumstantial): شعر بضعفٍ **يمنعه** (felt a weakness that was preventing him). **تناول** = to eat, to have (a meal) — Form VI: تَفاعَل, often implies 'taking from both sides'; here used as a refined synonym for أكل (to eat).

sentences.base_language: The hospital was two subway stops from his apartment.
sentences.target_language: كانت المستشفى على بُعد محطتَين بالمترو من شقته.
sentences.romanized: Kānat al-mustashfā ʿalā buʿdi maḥaṭṭatayn bi-l-mitrū min shaqatih.
sentences.grammarNote:
**على بُعد** = at a distance of: **بُعد** (distance) is a masdar from **بَعُد** (to be far). **على بُعد** + measure: **على بُعد كيلومترَين** (two kilometers away), **على بُعد خطوات** (a few steps away). This is the standard formal Arabic way to express measured distance.
**محطتَين** — dual form of محطة (station): Arabic has a productive dual suffix: **محطة** → **محطتان** (nom.) / **محطتَين** (gen./acc.). Here it follows **بُعد** in a genitive chain (iḍāfa), so it takes the genitive ending -ayn: **محطتَ**ين. Remember: dual nouns in iḍāfa drop the final ن: **محطتا المترو** (the two metro stations — nom.), **محطتَي المترو** (gen./acc.). Here the standalone dual is used since بُعد is not a noun but an indeclinable phrase.

sentences.base_language: At the reception, a nurse asked him: "How can I help you?"
sentences.target_language: عند مكتب الاستقبال، سألته ممرِّضةٌ: «كيف يمكنني مساعدتك؟»
sentences.romanized: ʿInda maktab al-istiqbāl, saʾalathu mumarriḍatun: «Kayfa yumkinunī musāʿadatuk?»
sentences.grammarNote:
**يمكنني + مصدر/أن** = I can, it is possible for me to: **يمكن** (impersonal: it is possible) + **ني** (suffix: for me/to me) + **مساعدة** (masdar: helping) + **ك** (you). The structure يمكنني is very common in MSA and formal speech: **يمكنني** الذهاب (I can go), **يمكنك** الاختيار (you can choose). Compare: **أستطيع** (I am able to — Form X of طوع, more personal). **يمكن** is impersonal and elegant; **أستطيع** is personal and direct.
**ممرِّضة** = nurse (female): from **رَضَع** (to suckle/nurse) via Form II مَرَّض (to nurse the sick). The **ة** (tāʾ marbūṭa) marks the feminine: **ممرِّض** (male nurse), **ممرِّضة** (female nurse). Note the tanwīn damm (ٌ) = indefinite nominative marker — the nurse is introduced here for the first time, so she is indefinite. After mention, she would take the definite article: **الممرِّضة**.

sentences.base_language: He replied: "My head and stomach hurt, and I feel completely exhausted."
sentences.target_language: أجاب: «يؤلمني رأسي وبطني، وأشعر بإرهاقٍ تام.»
sentences.romanized: Ajāba: «Yuʾlimunī raʾsī wa-baṭnī, wa-ashʿuru bi-irhāqin tāmm.»
sentences.grammarNote:
**يؤلمني** = hurts me: **يؤلم** (Form IV of أَلِم, to be in pain: آلَم = to cause pain) + **ني** (me). Form IV (**أفعل**) is typically causative: أَلِم (to feel pain) → آلَم (to cause pain). So يؤلمني = he/it causes pain to me → it hurts me. The subject **رأسي** (my head) follows the verb: يؤلمني **رأسي** (lit. my head pains me) — VSO order.
**إرهاق** = exhaustion: masdar from Form IV **أرهق** (to exhaust/overwhelm). Form IV masdar pattern إفعال: أرهق → **إرهاق** (exhaustion), أرسل → **إرسال** (sending), أعطى → **إعطاء** (giving). **تام** (complete, total) — note the pattern فاعل: **تام** (complete), **دائم** (permanent), **غائب** (absent). **إرهاق تام** = complete exhaustion — more formal than تعب شديد.

sentences.base_language: A doctor examined him and asked: "Do you exercise regularly? How is your diet?"
sentences.target_language: فحصه طبيبٌ وسأله: «هل تمارس الرياضة بانتظام؟ كيف نظامك الغذائي؟»
sentences.romanized: Faḥaṣahū ṭabībun wa-saʾalahu: «Hal tumārisu al-riyāḍa bi-intiẓām? Kayfa niẓāmuka al-ghidhāʾī?»
sentences.grammarNote:
**مارس** (Form III: فاعل) = to practice/engage in: **يمارس الرياضة** (practices/does sports/exercise). Form III (**فاعَل**) often implies a sustained or reciprocal activity: **مارس** (practice), **قاتل** (fight — reciprocal), **شارك** (participate). **الرياضة** here means both sports and physical exercise generally. **بانتظام** = regularly: **بـ** (with) + **انتظام** (regularity — masdar of Form VIII انتظم: to be regular/orderly). This بـ + masdar adverbial pattern is very common: **بسرعة** (quickly), **بهدوء** (calmly), **بعناية** (carefully).
**النظام الغذائي** = dietary system, diet: **نظام** (system/regimen) + **الغذائي** (dietary — adjective from غذاء, food/nutrition). The nisba adjective **غذائي** is formed from **غذاء** + **ي**: غذاء → **غذائي** (dietary/nutritional). This iḍāfa + nisba pattern is very common in formal Arabic: النظام **الصحي** (health system), النظام **التعليمي** (educational system).

sentences.base_language: He admitted he had been eating a lot of junk food and barely resting.
sentences.target_language: اعترف بأنه كان يأكل الكثير من الوجبات السريعة وبالكاد يرتاح.
sentences.romanized: Iʿtarafa bi-annahū kāna yaʾkulu al-kathīra min al-wajabāt al-sarīʿa wa-bil-kādi yartāḥ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اعترف** (Form VIII: افتعل) = to admit/confess: root ع-ر-ف (to know) → Form VIII **اعترف** (to acknowledge, to admit). Form VIII often carries a reflexive nuance: **اعترف** (acknowledged to himself/publicly), **اكتشف** (discovered — for oneself). **بأنه** = that he: **بـ** (with) + **أن** (that) + **ه** (he/his) — the bāʾ here introduces the content of the admission, equivalent to 'about the fact that.'
**الوجبات السريعة** = fast food (lit. the quick meals): **وجبة** (meal) → **وجبات** (meals — sound feminine plural: add **ات**). **السريعة** (quick/fast — feminine plural adjective) agrees in definiteness and gender: **الوجبات** (definite, feminine plural) → **السريعة** (definite, feminine plural). **بالكاد** = barely/hardly — a fixed adverbial expression: **بـ** (with) + **الكاد** (the difficulty/limit). Compare: **بالكاد** يمشي (can barely walk), **بالكاد** ينام (barely sleeps).

sentences.base_language: The doctor said: "You need more rest and a better diet. Drink plenty of water."
sentences.target_language: قال الطبيب: «تحتاج إلى مزيدٍ من الراحة ونظامٍ غذائيٍّ أفضل. اشرب الكثير من الماء.»
sentences.romanized: Qāla al-ṭabīb: «Taḥtāju ilā mazīdin min al-rāḥa wa-niẓāmin ghidhāʾiyyin afḍal. Ishrab al-kathīra min al-māʾ.»
sentences.grammarNote:
**احتاج إلى** = to need: **يحتاج** (Form VIII of حوج) + **إلى** (to): تحتاج **إلى** مزيد (you need more). This is the standard verb for 'need' in MSA. Compare: **يحتاج** (needs — impersonal use: يحتاج هذا إلى وقت = this needs time) vs **يحتاج إلى** (needs [something]). **مزيد من** = more of: **مزيد** (masdar of أزاد, to increase) + **من** + noun: مزيد **من** الراحة (more rest).
**اشرب** — Form I imperative of شَرِب (to drink): the imperative in Arabic is formed from the jussive (لم-form) by adding a helping vowel and dropping the prefix: يشرب → لا يشرَب (jussive: يشرَب) → **اشرب** (imperative — with hamzat al-waṣl). Imperative formation: remove the prefix (ي/ت) and add همزة الوصل if needed: **اكتب** (write), **اقرأ** (read), **اشرب** (drink). The vowel of the imperative hamza is determined by the vowel on the second radical.

sentences.base_language: He felt a little embarrassed, but also relieved to know it wasn't serious.
sentences.target_language: شعر ببعض الإحراج، لكنه شعر بارتياحٍ أيضًا حين علم أن الأمر ليس خطيرًا.
sentences.romanized: Shaʿara bi-baʿḍ al-iḥrāj, lākinnahū shaʿara bi-irtiyāḥin ayḍan ḥīna ʿalima anna al-amra laysa khaṭīran.
sentences.grammarNote:
**الإحراج** = embarrassment: masdar of Form IV **أحرج** (to embarrass, to put in an awkward position — from حَرَج, narrowness/difficulty). Form IV masdar pattern **إفعال**: أحرج → **إحراج** (embarrassment), أرسل → **إرسال** (sending). **ارتياح** = relief: masdar of Form VIII **ارتاح** (to feel at ease/rest — root ر-و-ح). Pattern: افتعال: ارتاح → **ارتياح** (relief/comfort). The bāʾ + masdar pattern **بارتياح** = with relief, in a relieved manner.
**ليس + اسم + خبر منصوب** = is not: **ليس الأمر خطيرًا** (the matter is not serious). **ليس** (negative copula — the negative of كان in the present) takes a nominative subject and accusative predicate: **ليس** الأمر **خطيرًا** (the matter is not serious — الأمر: nom., خطيرًا: acc.). Compare: **لا** + noun (nominal negation, simpler): الأمر **لا** خطير (non-standard in MSA — prefer ليس for formal contexts).

sentences.base_language: He left the hospital and found a grocery store nearby.
sentences.target_language: غادر المستشفى ووجد متجرًا للبقالة قريبًا منه.
sentences.romanized: Ghādara al-mustashfā wa-wajada matjaran lil-baqqāla qarīban minh.
sentences.grammarNote:
**غادر** (Form III: فاعل) = to leave/depart: **غادر** المكان (left the place). Form III of this root (غ-و-د-ر) carries the sense of departing from a place. Compare: **خرج من** (to go out of — Form I, simpler, can be used in more contexts): خرج **من** المستشفى (went out of the hospital). **غادر** is more formal and implies a purposeful departure; **خرج** is more general.
**متجر للبقالة** = grocery store: **متجر** (store — pattern مَفعَل/مَفعِل, a place of trade — from تَجَر, to trade) + **للبقالة** (for groceries/grocery trade — بَقَّالة from بقَّال, grocer). The **لـ** (for) creates a genitive link showing purpose: متجر **للبقالة** (a store for groceries). Compare: **سوبرماركت** (supermarket — loanword) used in everyday speech. **قريبًا منه** = near it/nearby — **قريبًا** is accusative (adverb of place), **منه** (from it — referring to the hospital).

sentences.base_language: He bought vegetables, fish, and fruit — no snacks this time.
sentences.target_language: اشترى خُضَرًا وسمكًا وفاكهةً — لا وجبات خفيفة هذه المرة.
sentences.romanized: Ishtarā khuḍaran wa-samakan wa-fākihatan — lā wajabāt khafīfa hādhihi al-marra.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اشترى** (Form VIII: افتعل) = to buy: root ش-ر-و → Form VIII **اشترى** (bought). Note that Form VIII verbs with root ending ي (like اشتر**ى**) conjugate as defective verbs: اشترى (he bought), اشترَت (she bought), اشترَيتُ (I bought). The Form I is **شرى** (also to buy — rare in MSA; **باع** is more common for 'sell'). **خُضَر** — broken plural of **خُضرة** (vegetable/green thing): the pattern **فُعَل** for broken plurals: خُضرة → **خُضَر**. Arabic broken plurals must be memorized — they do not follow a single predictable pattern.
**لا + نكرة** = no ~ (complete negation): **لا وجبات خفيفة** (no snacks — lit. no light meals). **لا** of negation before an indefinite noun negates its existence entirely (النفي الجنسي): **لا إله إلا الله** (there is no god but God), **لا مشكلة** (no problem). The noun after **لا** is typically in the accusative without tanwīn if it is a single definable concept, or with tanwīn if individual. **وجبات خفيفة** = light meals/snacks: **خفيفة** (light — feminine plural adj.) agrees with **وجبات** (feminine plural noun).

sentences.base_language: Back home, he made a simple soup with vegetables and chicken.
sentences.target_language: حين عاد إلى المنزل، أعدَّ حساءً بسيطًا بالخضروات والدجاج.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna ʿāda ilā al-manzil, aʿadda ḥasāʾan basīṭan bil-khuḍruwāt wal-dajāj.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أعدَّ** (Form IV: أفعل) = to prepare: root ع-د-د → Form IV **أعدَّ** (to prepare/make ready). The doubled root ددعَ shows gemination: أعدَّ is written with a shadda: أعـ + دَّ. Form IV is typically causative: عَدَّ (to count) → أعدَّ (to prepare/make ready). Common Form IV verbs: **أكل → أطعم** (to feed), **سهل → أسهل** (to facilitate), **عدَّ → أعدَّ** (to prepare).
**حساء** = soup: a masdar used as a noun, from the root ح-س-و (to sip). Pattern: فَعَال. **بسيط** = simple (not compound, not complicated) — the same root as **بَسَط** (to spread out): **بسيط** (flat, simple — lit. spread out). Compare: **سهل** (easy — simpler, about difficulty level) vs **بسيط** (simple — about complexity or elaborateness). **بـ** of accompaniment: **بالخضروات** (with the vegetables) — the same **بـ** seen in يشعر بالتعب.

sentences.base_language: While cooking, he called his friend and told her about the doctor's visit.
sentences.target_language: بينما كان يطبخ، اتصل بصديقته وأخبرها عن زيارته للطبيب.
sentences.romanized: Baynamā kāna yaṭbukhu, ittaṣala bi-ṣadīqatih wa-akhbarahā ʿan ziyāratihī lil-ṭabīb.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اتصل بـ** (Form VIII: افتعل) = to contact, to call: **اتصل بـ** + person (the preposition **بـ** is part of the verb's pattern). Compare: **اتصل بصديقته** (called his friend — phone) vs **تواصل مع** (communicated with — broader). Form VIII of وصل (to connect/arrive): **اتصل** (to connect/call — reflexive nuance of connecting oneself). Note that **اتصل بـ** takes **بـ** not **إلى**: اتصل **بـ**ه (not *إليه*).
**أخبرها عن** = told her about: **أخبر** (Form IV: to inform/tell) + **ها** (her, direct object suffix) + **عن** (about). Form IV of خَبَر (news/information): أخبر (to inform). Compare: **أخبر** (informed, told — implies giving information) vs **قال لـ** (said to — direct speech). **زيارته للطبيب** — possessive + purpose: **زيارة** (visit) + **ه** (his) + **للطبيب** (to/for the doctor — لـ + ال contracted to للـ).

sentences.base_language: His friend laughed and said: "I always told you to take better care of yourself!"
sentences.target_language: ضحكت صديقته وقالت: «كنت دائمًا أقول لك أن تعتني بصحتك بشكلٍ أفضل!»
sentences.romanized: Ḍaḥikat ṣadīqatuh wa-qālat: «Kuntu dāʾiman aqulu laka an taʿtaniya bi-ṣiḥḥatika bi-shaklin afḍal!»
sentences.grammarNote:
**اعتنى بـ** (Form VIII: افتعل) = to take care of: root ع-ن-ي → Form VIII **اعتنى** (to care for, to look after). + **بـ** (with/for): اعتنى **بـ**صحته (took care of his health). This verb requires the bāʾ preposition. Compare: **اهتم بـ** (to care about, be concerned with — also Form VIII): اهتم **بـ**عمله (cared about his work). **اعتنى** tends to mean active care/maintenance; **اهتم** is more about concern or interest.
**كنت أقول لك أن + مضارع منصوب** = I used to tell you to: **كنت** (I was) + **أقول** (I say — present, for habitual past) + **لك** (to you) + **أن** + **تعتنيَ** (you care — subjunctive/مضارع منصوب). After **أن** in a command/wish context, the verb is in the subjunctive (منصوب): **أن تعتنيَ** (that you take care). This use of **كان + مضارع** for past habit is essential: كنت **أذهب** كل يوم (I used to go every day).

sentences.base_language: After eating, he felt much better — his energy was returning.
sentences.target_language: بعد الأكل، شعر بتحسُّنٍ كبير — كانت طاقته تعود إليه.
sentences.romanized: Baʿda al-akl, shaʿara bi-taḥassunin kabīr — kānat ṭāqatuhū taʿūdu ilayh.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تحسُّن** = improvement: masdar of Form V **تحسَّن** (to improve — intransitive). Form V (**تفعَّل**) is the reflexive/intransitive counterpart of Form II: **حسَّن** (to improve something — transitive) → **تحسَّن** (to become improved — intransitive). Pattern: تفعُّل: **تحسُّن** (improvement), **تطوُّر** (development), **تقدُّم** (progress). **شعر بتحسُّن كبير** = felt a great improvement — the bāʾ introduces the content of the feeling.
**كانت طاقته تعود إليه** = his energy was returning to him: **كانت** (was — feminine, agreeing with طاقة) + **طاقته** (his energy — subject, feminine) + **تعود** (returns — present, for past progressive) + **إليه** (to him). **طاقة** (energy — root ط-و-ق: capacity, ability) — feminine noun, hence **كانت**. **إليه** = to him: **إلى** + **ه** (him). Note: **عاد إلى** = returned to (complete return), but **تعود إليه** = was returning to him (gradual process).

sentences.base_language: He lay down and rested for a couple of hours.
sentences.target_language: استلقى واسترح لساعتَين.
sentences.romanized: Istalqā wa-istarāḥa li-sāʿatayn.
sentences.grammarNote:
**استلقى** (Form X: استفعل) = to lie down: root ل-ق-و → Form X **استلقى** (to lie down — lit. to seek a lying position). Form X often carries the meaning of 'to seek' or 'to consider oneself': **استلقى** (sought to lie down/lay down), **استقبل** (to receive/face), **استفاد** (to benefit). The Form X of this root is the standard MSA verb for lying down.
**لساعتَين** — dual with the preposition **لـ**: **لـ** (for — duration) + **ساعتَين** (two hours — dual genitive). The dual in Arabic ends in **-ayn** (gen./acc.) or **-ān** (nom.): **ساعتَان** (two hours — nom.), **ساعتَين** (gen./acc.). After prepositions (which require the genitive), the dual is always **-ayn**: لـ**ساعتَين** (for two hours), في **يومَين** (in two days). The dual is used productively in Arabic for pairs and twos — much more so than in most other languages.

sentences.base_language: By evening, his mood had completely improved and he felt calm and content.
sentences.target_language: وبحلول المساء، كان مزاجه قد تحسَّن تمامًا وكان يشعر بهدوءٍ ورضا.
sentences.romanized: Wa-bi-ḥulūl al-masāʾ, kāna mazājuh qad taḥassana tamāman wa-kāna yashʿuru bi-hudūʾin wa-riḍā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**بحلول** = by the arrival of, by the time of: **بـ** (with/at) + **حلول** (arrival/onset — masdar of حلَّ, to arrive/settle). **بحلول + الوقت** is a very common time expression in MSA: **بحلول** الصباح (by morning), **بحلول** الشهر القادم (by next month). Compare: **عند حلول** (upon the arrival of) and **حين** (when — for simultaneous events).
**كان + قد + ماضٍ** = had done (pluperfect): **كان** مزاجه **قد** تحسَّن (his mood had improved). **قد** before a past verb in a كان context creates the pluperfect: كان **قد** وصل (had arrived), كانت **قد** فعلت (had done). Without كان, **قد** + past tense emphasises completion or certainty: **قد** وصل (he has already arrived!). **رضا** = contentment, satisfaction: masdar of رضيَ (to be satisfied/content). Compare: **رضا** (the state of being content) vs **ارتياح** (relief from tension).

sentences.base_language: He promised himself to take better care of his body from now on.
sentences.target_language: وعد نفسه بالاهتمام بصحة جسده بشكلٍ أفضل من الآن فصاعدًا.
sentences.romanized: Waʿada nafsahū bil-ihtimām bi-ṣiḥḥat jasadih bi-shaklin afḍal min al-ān faṣāʿidan.
sentences.grammarNote:
**وعد** + مفعول به + بـ = to promise someone something: **وعد نفسه بـ** (promised himself to do — lit. promised his self with the-caring). **وعد** (to promise) takes a direct object (the person promised) and **بـ** + masdar (the promise): وعد **أمَّه** بالعودة (promised his mother he would return). Here **نفسه** (his self — reflexive direct object) is the person promised.
**من الآن فصاعدًا** = from now on: **من الآن** (from now) + **فصاعدًا** (and upward/onward — from صَعِد, to ascend). **فصاعدًا** is an adverb of degree/direction always used in the expression **من X فصاعدًا** (from X onwards): من يوم واحد فصاعدًا (from one day onwards), من الآن فصاعدًا (from now on). The **فـ** is a connective 'and then,' and **صاعدًا** (ascending/upward) gives the sense of going forward in time or scale.
